text: [serious] That video was AI-generated. Virat Kohli never made it.
[warning] Scammers used deepfake technology — AI that can copy anyone's face and voice from existing videos.
[clear] The channel was created 4 days ago. Real celebrities don't do giveaways through random links.
[focused] Your UPI PIN was the only thing they needed.
[serious] Scammers build fake urgency — 'only 87 spots' — specifically so you share it before thinking.
[grounded] This is exactly what always happens.
[warning] Paying tells them it's working. They don't stop — they ask for more.
[focused] The content they threatened you with? Almost certainly AI-generated and fake.
[serious] They send the same message to hundreds of students. Paying never ends it.
[firm] Don't engage. Every reply tells them the account is active and the person is scared.
[serious] They use that to push harder.
[confident] Silence and reporting is stronger than any reply.
[focused] They went to Free Fire's login page and typed YOUR username.
[clear] Free Fire sent a verification code — to YOU, the real owner.
[serious] They pretended it was a mistake.
[warning] That code was the key to YOUR account.
[grounded] There is no 'accidental number'. That story doesn't exist.
[firm] Do not engage. If an OTP came to your phone, it is for your account.
[clear] They can sign up with their own number without your involvement.
[serious] Replying keeps the scam active — there is no accidental number story.
[focused] Someone found out your friend's name and used it.
[grounded] That's all it took.
[serious] They knew that you'd trust Kabir. So they became Kabir.
[warning] The name was real. The number wasn't.
[serious] They'll say 'I told you, it's Kabir, just send it.'
[grounded] Asking for proof in a panic situation rarely works — they'll have an answer.
[confident] The only real verification is calling his actual number.
[clear] There was no investigation. No software detected. No ban.
[serious] The threat was fiction. The urgency was the weapon.
[warning] 'Reply in 15 minutes' was designed specifically to stop you from thinking clearly.
[focused] Krafton has full access to their own servers. They will NEVER need your password.
[firm] No real company ever will.
[warning] They now know your email.
[focused] They'll try it on 10 other sites.
[serious] Partial information is still information.
[grounded] Half a key can still open a lock.
